verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A encodes the inducible cytosolic Hsp70 chaperone, canonically intracellular. However, robust evidence documents cell-surface display under non-baseline conditions: (1) Cancer ecto-Hsp70 is well-established — tumor cells constitutively expose Hsp70 on the outer leaflet, making it a validated target for NK-cell recognition, antibody-based cancer therapeutics (e.g., cmHsp70.1 antibody), and CAR-T approaches; (2) Stress/heat shock and ICD (apoptosis, immunogenic cell death) induce PM translocation of Hsp70, where it acts as a DAMPs signal and 'eat-me' signal on the outer leaflet; (3) Hsp70 is also released in exosomes but that does not itself confer surface accessibility. No stable TM domain or GPI anchor exists; the cancer/stress ecto-pool is the surface story. This fits cell_state_induced under contextual. The protein body is accessible from the extracellular face specifically in tumor and stressed/dying cells.
